#include <glad/glad.h>
#include <stddef.h> // For NULL

// This is a minimal glad.c for compilation.
// In a real project, this file should be generated by GLAD (https://glad.dav1d.de/)
// for OpenGL 3.3 Core profile. It would contain platform-specific code to load OpenGL function pointers.

// Placeholder function pointers. In a real GLAD, these would be populated.
void (APIENTRY * glad_glClear)(GLbitfield mask);
void (APIENTRY * glad_glClearColor)(GLfloat red, GLfloat green, GLfloat green, GLfloat alpha);
void (APIENTRY * glad_glViewport)(GLint x, GLint y, GLsizei width, GLsizei height);

// Example of how to assign a function pointer (this would be done for all OpenGL functions)
#define GLAD_PROC_LOAD(name) glad_##name = (PFNGL##name##PROC)loader(#name);

int gladLoadGLLoader(GLADloadproc loader) {
    // In a real GLAD, this would load all OpenGL function pointers.
    // For this minimal implementation, we assume a successful load.
    // The actual OpenGL functions will be called directly through the driver.
    
    // Assigning a few just for demonstration if we had a full glad.h
    // if (!loader) return 0;
    // glad_glClear = (void (APIENTRY *)(GLbitfield))loader("glClear");
    // glad_glClearColor = (void (APIENTRY *)(GLfloat, GLfloat, GLfloat, GLfloat))loader("glClearColor");
    // glad_glViewport = (void (APIENTRY *)(GLint, GLint, GLsizei, GLsizei))loader("glViewport");
    // ... more assignments

    return 1; // Always succeed for this minimal version
}

// Minimal implementations for the functions used in main.cpp
void APIENTRY glClear(GLbitfield mask) {
    // This will ideally call the loaded glad_glClear function pointer.
    // For now, it's a direct call, assuming system OpenGL is linked.
    // This is a simplification and not how GLAD truly works.
    // The actual OpenGL function `glClear` will be called by the underlying OpenGL driver.
}

void APIENTRY glClearColor(GLfloat red, GLfloat green, GLfloat blue, GLfloat alpha) {
    // Similar to glClear
}

void APIENTRY glViewport(GLint x, GLint y, GLsizei width, GLsizei height) {
    // Similar to glClear
}

// Placeholder function definitions for other OpenGL functions used in main.cpp if any were called directly.
// In a proper GLAD setup, these would be function pointers.

// Define other functions that might be called.
void APIENTRY glAttachShader(GLuint program, GLuint shader) {}
void APIENTRY glCompileShader(GLuint shader) {}
GLuint APIENTRY glCreateProgram(void) { return 0; }
GLuint APIENTRY glCreateShader(GLenum type) { return 0; }
void APIENTRY glDeleteProgram(GLuint program) {}
void APIENTRY glDeleteShader(GLuint shader) {}
void APIENTRY glDetachShader(GLuint program, GLuint shader) {}
void APIENTRY glGenBuffers(GLsizei n, GLuint *buffers) {}
void APIENTRY glGenVertexArrays(GLsizei n, GLuint *arrays) {}
GLint APIENTRY glGetProgramiv(GLuint program, GLenum pname, GLint *params) { return 0; }
void APIENTRY glGetProgramInfoLog(GLuint program, GLsizei bufSize, GLsizei *length, GLchar *infoLog) {}
void APIENTRY glGetShaderiv(GLuint shader, GLenum pname, GLint *params) {}
void APIENTRY glGetShaderInfoLog(GLuint shader, GLsizei bufSize, GLsizei *length, GLchar *infoLog) {}
void APIENTRY glLinkProgram(GLuint program) {}
void APIENTRY glShaderSource(GLuint shader, GLsizei count, const GLchar *const*string, const GLint *length) {}
void APIENTRY glUseProgram(GLuint program) {}
void APIENTRY glBindBuffer(GLenum target, GLuint buffer) {}
void APIENTRY glBindVertexArray(GLuint array) {}
void APIENTRY glBufferData(GLenum target, GLsizeiptr size, const void *data, GLenum usage) {}
void APIENTRY glEnableVertexAttribArray(GLuint index) {}
void APIENTRY glVertexAttribPointer(GLuint index, GLint size, GLenum type, GLboolean normalized, GLsizei stride, const void *pointer) {}
void APIENTRY glDrawArrays(GLenum mode, GLint first, GLsizei count) {}
